  "title": "The Foundations of Probabilistic Language: Information Theory and Markovian Chains",
  "scope": "Establish the mathematical bedrock of NLP by exploring Claude Shannon's Information Theory and the concept of entropy as a measure of predictability in language. parsed.title: The Foundations of Probabilistic Language: Information Theory and Markovian Chains
parsed.scope: Establish the mathematical bedrock of NLP by exploring Claude Shannon's Information Theory and the concept of entropy as a measure of predictability in language. Transition into the first formal computational models of text: Markov Chains and n-gram models, explaining how they treat language as a stochastic process governed by local dependency.

runs.rawOutput:
{
  "done": false,
  "title": "The Stochastic Nature of Language: Shannon Entropy and Markovian Chains",
  "scope": "Establish the mathematical foundation of language as information. This section covers Shannon's definition of entropy as the limit of predictability and the transition to Markov Chains/n-grams as the first algorithmic attempts to model sequential probability in text, framed through the lens of a Senior Engineer's understanding of state machines and data compression.",
  "concepts": ["Information Theory and Shannon Entropy", "Markov Chains and n-gram models"],
  "estimatedSections": 3
}

parsed.title: The Stochastic Nature of Language: Shannon Entropy and Markovian Chains
parsed.scope: Establish the mathematical foundation of language as information. This section covers Shannon's definition of entropy as the limit of predictability and the transition to Markov Chains/n-grams as the first algorithmic attempts to model sequential probability in text, framed through the lens of a Senior Engineer's understanding of state machines and data compression.

runs.rawOutput:
{
  "done": false,
  "title": "The Mathematical Birth of Language: Information Theory and Markovian Dynamics",
  "scope": "Establish the first-principles of language as a stochastic process. This section introduces Claude Shannon's Information Theory to quantify language uncertainty (Entropy) and the transition into Markov Chains as the foundational generative models for sequence prediction.",
  "concepts": ["Information Theory and Shannon Entropy", "Markov Chains and n-gram models"],
  "estimatedSections": 3
}

parsed.title: The Mathematical Birth of Language: Information Theory and Markovian Dynamics
parsed.scope: Establish the first-principles of language as a stochastic process. This section introduces Claude Shannon's Information Theory to quantify language uncertainty (Entropy) and the transition into Markov Chains as the foundational generative models for sequence prediction.
